name: Patient H
presenting_complaint: Right-sided chest wall pain after falling off a ladder while clearing gutters this morning; pain 8/10 on inspiration.
history: Atrial fibrillation on apixaban (last dose this morning). Hypertension. Independent. Tender over right 5th-8th ribs anteriorly with crepitus. Reduced air entry right base. AVPU=A. Anxious but cooperative.
true_diagnosis: Multiple right rib fractures with traumatic haemothorax

trajectory.state: Splinting respirations, RR 22, SpO2 94% room air. SBP 102 — borderline. On apixaban so bleeding risk amplified.

Chest X-ray: right 5th-8th rib fractures + blunting of costophrenic angle. Needs cardiothoracic surgery consult AND orthopaedics for rib fixation discussion AND haematology input on anticoagulant reversal.
Repeat SpO2 91% on room air, SBP 94. Bedside ultrasound confirms haemothorax. Chest drain insertion required. Decision on apixaban reversal (andexanet vs PCC) needs haematology.

Post chest drain: 600mL blood out. Cardiothoracic input on whether ongoing drainage warrants thoracotomy. Ortho/anaesthetics for serratus plane block analgesia. Multi-team coordination is the bottleneck.

red_herrings: Initial SpO2 94% looks acceptable but is dropping
Pain may be attributed to chest wall alone, missing haemothorax
